multiplechoiceresponse.label: When comparing the Grad-CAM overlays for islet and harbor, the highlighted regions differ noticeably. Which interpretation is most consistent with the Grad-CAM visualizations?

choicegroup.choice: The model emphasizes isolated land masses surrounded by water for islet, while focusing more on coastal infrastructure and shoreline regions for harbor.
The model highlights the same image regions for both classes, suggesting it cannot distinguish between them.
The model attends primarily to the center of the image regardless of the class being evaluated.
Grad-CAM highlights arbitrary regions that are unrelated to the scene label.

div.p: Grad-CAM highlights the regions that most strongly support a given class score. When the overlays for islet and harbor light up different regions, it means the model is relying on different visual cues for each category. For islet, the model focuses on compact land areas surrounded by water, reflecting the defining visual characteristics of an islet. For harbor, the attention shifts toward broader shoreline regions and man-made coastal structures. The difference in highlighted areas across these two overlays shows that the model is using distinct spatial cues to differentiate between related but semantically different scene categories.
